title: Localization phase diagram of the Hexagonal Lattice with irrational magnetic flux
authors: Qi Gao, Shuo Zhang, Wei Chen
abstract: We study the Hofstadter model on a hexagonal lattice with irrational magnetic flux in this work. The Hofstadter model of the square lattice with irrational flux has been solved mathematically by Avila in his Fields medal work. However, this theory is usually not applicable to lattices with internal degrees of freedom, such as spin or sub-lattices. In this work, we show that for the hexagonal lattice with only nearest neighbor hopping, the system can still be characterized by a 2*2 transfer matrix and solved exactly by Avila$'$s global theory of Avila although this lattice has two sub-lattices. We obtained the exact localization phase diagram of the hexagonal lattice with irrational flux by this theory, which reveals three pure phases, that is, the extended, localized and critical states but no mobility edge due to the chiral symmetry. We used the renormalization group (RG) theory to verify these results, which can determine part of the phase diagram. We then computed the fractal dimension of the remaining part numerically. The results from both the RG theory and numerical analysis confirmed the phase diagram we get from Avila$'$s global theory.
summary: This paper provides an exact localization phase diagram for the Hofstadter model on a hexagonal lattice with irrational magnetic flux. By leveraging Avila's global theory, the authors identify extended, localized, and critical phases while proving that chiral symmetry prevents the formation of a mobility edge. The results are further validated through renormalization group theory and numerical fractal dimension calculations.
main_problem: Determining the localization phase diagram of the Hofstadter model on a hexagonal lattice subject to irrational magnetic flux.
main_result: The study reveals three pure phases—extended, localized, and critical—and demonstrates the absence of a mobility edge due to chiral symmetry.
method: The authors apply Avila's global theory of transfer matrices, supplemented by renormalization group (RG) theory and numerical computation of fractal dimensions.
model_or_system: The Hofstadter model on a hexagonal lattice with two sub-lattices and nearest-neighbor hopping under an irrational magnetic flux.
key_observables: Localization phase diagram, fractal dimension, and presence/absence of mobility edges.
important_parameters: Irrational magnetic flux and nearest-neighbor hopping amplitude.
main_assumptions: The system is restricted to nearest-neighbor hopping, which allows the reduction of the sub-lattice problem to a 2x2 transfer matrix.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem by referencing Avila's work on square lattices, applies Avila's global theory to the hexagonal lattice, uses RG theory and numerical analysis for verification, and concludes with the resulting phase diagram.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
